Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Pension Plan
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Pension Plan

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Pension Plan to provide that the consent of at least two thirds of the provinces that do not provide a comprehensive pension plan is required before a province may adopt such a plan.

Whereas Canadians living and working in provinces in which the Canada Pension Plan applies may incur changes to their pension benefits as a result of a province withdrawing from the Canada Pension Plan;
And whereas Parliament believes it is necessary to ensure that Canadians remain entitled to the pension benefits that they have worked for;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 3 of the Canada Pension Plan is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Regulations under subsection (2) may only be made if the lieutenant governor in council of each of at least two thirds of the provinces that are not provinces providing a comprehensive pension plan, having in total not less than two thirds of the population of all those provinces, has signified the consent of that province.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on the 180th day after the day on which it receives royal assent.
